data.epi_content:
After drinking a lot of coffee and eating chocolate, time passed quickly.

Normally, I would have stubbornly found a seat in the classroom, but this time I just gave up quickly.

"What should I do? This is quite enjoyable, but I'm getting tired of it now."

It's natural to get sick of chocolate if you keep eating it.

Especially when it's fancy chocolate made for cleansing the palate after meals, or in other words, unnecessarily bitter chocolate made to suit the tastes of nobles.

The sun has completely set.

When I open the window, I can't hear the occasional laughter of children or the chirping of birds.

Though it's just a closed room where I'm alone while everyone is asleep, I take off my clothes, exposing my naked body.

Then I entered the bathroom.

"Mmm."

When my bare feet touched the bathroom floor, I felt something creeping up my spine along with a slightly cool sensation.

I touch the part of the bathtub where the back of my neck would have been.

"Is this where I shot wrong?

Maybe it would have been better to just do it with one."

I might have died painfully by clumsily mixing two different methods.

If I had just blown my head off at once, there wouldn't have been any problem.

What nonsense about dignity.

A corpse is just a piece of meat.

Even though I think this way now, this body would never accept such thoughts.

I fill the bathtub with warm water.

Then I stand up and look in the mirror.

I don't feel particularly embarrassed or ashamed.

Even if I recall who I was, what kind of person I used to be, and how I used to think, when I see this appearance, I have no choice but to accept it.

A healthy—no, slightly weak but still decent body, vibrant silver hair, pale skin, and lifeless eyes with barely visible focus.

A chest with no remarkable features and slightly curved lower body.

I thought there might be something different when I took off my clothes, but there doesn't seem to be.

No visible scars or magic traces that supposedly appear when exposed to strong magical power.

I touched various parts of my body with my palm.

I feel a soft touch along with sensation.

I was alive.

After staring at the mirror for a long time, appreciating this lady's naked body, I noticed the bathtub was already full.

I get into the warm bathtub and close my eyes.

And then I fell asleep.

.

.

.

.

.

Good morning! If you were to ask me about this strong and powerful morning, I would...

Let's stop. Even when I try to come up with amusing monologues, nothing really comes to mind.

The thoughts I attempted to escape from the gloomy, depressing mood were buried deep in my brain.

"Ah, I don't want to go."

Since my eyes are open, it must be morning.

My body feels languid from being in the bathtub for several hours.

I feel a bit dizzy too.

Strangely, my skin isn't pruned at all.

Only water droplets are lightly clinging to it.

I get out of the bathtub.

I feel slightly dizzy.

"Ouch."
data.epi_content2:
I lost my balance and fell.

I seem to have twisted my leg a bit.

Well, it doesn't matter.

It's just a little pain.

I want to walk with a limp to minimize the pain, but my body refuses.

At times like this, I would feel slightly depressed.

Still, I look in the mirror, make myself as presentable as possible, and get dressed.

After putting on my shoes, I head to the classroom while hearing people whispering about me.

Most of the whispers were about how my father had passed away, how my brother had been beheaded as a traitor, how the family was ruined, how that half-wit might take over everything, and what would happen to me.

At first, whenever I heard such whispers, my body would tremble slightly, but now I can ignore them without feeling anything.

I'd say that's quite an improvement.

When I enter the classroom, Lydia approaches me with that annoying face, wearing a slight sneer, and says:

"Oh, um. People are talking a lot about you.

What can I say? There's not a single person in the Academy who doesn't know about your pitiful situation."

"Indeed."

"You're quite composed. Considering how passionately angry you were at that commoner girl when she took Evan from you, this is somewhat unexpected."

"Would anything change if I expressed my emotions to you or Vivian?"

"I suppose not. Nothing will change, nor will what I'm about to do.

Miss, I'll come see you in a bit.

During difficult times, one should gather with close friends and share friendship!"

I wanted to say "fuck off," but it seems this lady's mouth doesn't let out curses unless pushed to the extreme.

That's what makes it even more terrible.

Not being able to say what I want to say or move how I want to move.

Sometimes I feel like I'm trapped in a pretty etiquette doll.

It must be around 10 o'clock.

The teacher keeps glancing at me nervously during the lesson.

After a while, he says that today's class will end here and tells everyone to study on their own before going to lunch.

The children in the class either return to their dormitory rooms or go for a walk with pleasant expressions.

I tried to leave naturally, hiding among the crowd of people, but it was futile as someone grabbed my arm.

It was a boy whose name I didn't know, someone I had never spoken to before.

Rather than trying to speak to him, I tried to shake off his arm with force, but my weak strength made it impossible to break free.

And Lydia approaches me with an intimidating atmosphere, bringing the children she controls.

"Miss Erica, no matter what, you're still a noble person.

Don't you think running away is a bit inappropriate? If it were me, I'd be terribly embarrassed, hee."

"Well, a group surrounding and threatening an individual seems quite ugly too, so let's call it even."

"Your tongue is still sharp. Well, both you and I have spent our lives learning how to use our mouths, so it's natural.

But most words that come out of one's mouth are terribly pathetic without the authority or power to back them up.

Hey, you can let her go now."

While we were having this conversation, the children surrounded me to prevent me from escaping.

The boy who had grabbed my arm left with a somewhat disgusted expression, but I'm much weaker than girls my age.

Just because one person who was holding my wrist disappeared doesn't mean I can escape.

And thanks to twisting my ankle this morning, running is impossible, and even walking properly is a challenge.

Well, I'm doomed.

"In contrast, no matter what evil deed one does, if there's a background supporting it, it becomes a good thing.

That's why I love this kind of power. Thanks to it, I can even torment a miss like you who would have grown up so beautifully."

After saying that, Lydia taps her mouth slightly in a mocking manner.

"Oh my, my true feelings slipped out. What should I do?

Girls, the miss seems tired. Let's give her a shoulder to lean on."
data.epi_content3:
At those words, rather than offering a shoulder to lean on, I'm essentially restrained as if being taken into custody by the police and dragged along.

"Hmm, what should we do?

I can't think of anything good."

Lydia taps her chin with her finger for a while and then asks me a question.

"Miss, I'm curious. What kind of punishment did you receive when you did something wrong as a child?"

"Well, I've never done anything wrong, so I wouldn't know."

It's the truth.

Neither my father nor my mother ever punished me.

At those words, Lydia's eyes curve into a smile.

"Is that so? When I did something wrong as a child, my mother would lock me in the closet.

It seems like an appropriate punishment for a foolish girl who doesn't know her place."

"Ha, heh. Heh."

"What's so funny?"

"It's just funny that you... never mind, you wouldn't understand even if I explained."

Isn't living like that exhausting?

Going back and forth like a bat, attaching yourself here and there seems quite tiring.

Choosing who to despise also seems quite exhausting.

Not that I'm in a position to care.

Well, once again, she speaks without a single word or term out of place.

Then she'll casually say things I really don't want to hear.

"You really don't know your place. Well, I suppose your handsome—

Oh, I should say 'formerly handsome' now.

Anyway, did your brother also lose his head because he didn't know his place and acted out of line?"

Did my expression harden?

Lydia places her hand on my mouth, pinches the corner of my lips, and forces them upward.

"Don't look at me like that. It's scary.

Smile, smile. That's how you stay happy.

Whether your family died or your house fell, shouldn't you smile to enjoy life?"

Lydia has the children bring me to the front of a locker.

A small, narrow locker with a height so low that it's impossible to stretch one's legs.

My body trembled.

It's terrifying.

Being confined always evokes a terrible feeling.

Especially when it's a space so narrow that escape is impossible.

The fear of confinement is beyond imagination.

"Since there's no closet in sight, I'll have to use this.

Ahaha, don't tremble, don't tremble.

It's a space big enough for a small and cute girl like you."

Lydia's smiling face suddenly turns cold.

And the words she utters are just four syllables.

"Put her in."

"Ah, no."

"What can you do if you don't want to?"

Even if I struggle and move my body around, it doesn't budge.

The children lift my body, fold it so I'm crouching, and put me into the locker.

With my face buried in my knees, I'm forcibly put into the locker.

And the door closes.

It was dark, and I couldn't move.

"You can't do anything."
